# [enhancement] Promote GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD to aiConfig.data for human tuning

## Context

PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) (resolves #10035) introduced `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.8` as a named constant inside `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine.mjs` with documented derivation from the `ConceptService` weight formula. Deferred config-lifting to this follow-up ticket to keep the refactor's scope focused on the architectural change (regex + LLM → graph traversal).

The threshold governs when a concept lacking an `EXPLAINED_BY` edge emits a `[GUIDE_GAP]`. Default value `0.8` means "at least tier-1 baseline priority" — every tier-1 concept without a guide qualifies; tier-2/3 concepts qualify only if uniqueness + coverage-deficit bump them above. The derivation is stable, but future ontology growth (post-#10050/#10036/#10037) may reveal the default needs tuning without triggering a code change.

## The Fix

1. Promote the constant to `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` in `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`
2. Preserve the default value (`0.8`)
3. Preserve the derivation comment — move it from the constant's JSDoc to the config-template comment so curators see the rationale when tuning
4. Import + consume the config value in `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps`
5. Run `node ./buildScripts/ai/initServerConfigs.mjs` will pick up the new config key on install — verify no regeneration needed on existing installs (should be fine since it's additive)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` exists in `config.template.mjs` with default `0.8` + derivation comment
- [ ] `GapInferenceEngine` reads the value from config, not from a file-local constant
- [ ] Existing PR #10084 tests still pass (regression guard)
- [ ] New test: threshold override via config produces different gap-emission behavior

`62d6f155-e57f-4279-9b59-36c9e4ecbc5e` — flagged as out-of-scope in PR [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084) body

- Parent epic: #10030
- Origin PR: [#10084](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10084)
- Weight formula: `ConceptService.calculateWeight` — `tier_score (0.8/0.5/0.3) + uniqueness (0.2) + coverage_deficit (0.3)`

- 2026-04-19T15:19:00Z @tobiu referenced in commit `db5bdae` - "feat(ai): config-lift guideGapWeightThreshold to aiConfig.data (#10086)

Promotes the previously file-local `GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.8` constant
in `GapInferenceEngine` to `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold`, making the
weight gate curator-tunable without a code change. Default value preserved
(`0.8`); the derivation comment moves from the const JSDoc to the config
template next to the value itself, where tuners will see it.

## Scope

- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`: adds `guideGapWeightThreshold:
  Number(process.env.NEO_GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD) || 0.8` after `decayFactor`
  (both graph-pipeline behavior floats — semantic grouping). JSDoc carries the
  full derivation from ConceptService.calculateWeight plus the post-#10087
  context (threshold now gates three signals, not just GUIDE_GAP — historical
  prefix retained per ticket AC).
- `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine.mjs`: removes the file-local const,
  imports `Memory_Config as aiConfig` via the services SDK, caches the
  threshold once per cycle at the top of `inferConceptGraphGaps` so the live
  config value is consulted (tests can mutate it; module-load capture would
  miss runtime overrides). Class + method JSDoc updated to reference the
  config path and explain the historical `guideGap*` prefix (introduced in
  #10035 for GUIDE_GAP alone; widened in #10087 to gate all three concept-
  graph signals — rename considered but scoped out to keep this PR tight).

**Parity note:** the gitignored local `config.mjs` must mirror the template
addition for tests to pick up the key. Updated locally in this worktree but
not committed (by design — the template is the source of truth for the
shipped repo; each checkout's `config.mjs` is derived manually or via
`bootstrapWorktree.mjs` on worktree creation).

## Tests (41/41 stable × 5 runs)

New test `threshold override via aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold
changes emission behavior (#10086)`:
- Plants a tier-2 concept with weight 0.5 (below default 0.8 threshold).
- Overrides `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold = 0.3` inside try/finally.
- Asserts the concept now emits both `[GUIDE_GAP]` and `[ORPHAN_CONCEPT]`,
  proving the config is read at gate time (not captured at module load).
- Restores the original value in `finally`.

The existing "skip low-weight concepts" test (weight 0.3 with default 0.8)
remains and now acts as the counterpart — default config path.

## AC checklist

- [x] `aiConfig.data.guideGapWeightThreshold` exists in `config.template.mjs`
      with default 0.8 + derivation comment (moved from GapInferenceEngine)
- [x] `GapInferenceEngine` reads the value from config, not from a file-local
      constant — verified via runtime-override test
- [x] Existing tests pass (40 → 41 total, all green × 5 consecutive runs)
- [x] New test proves threshold override via config produces different
      gap-emission behavior
